text.title: pnpm Setup and Configuration
text.summary: Installation, .npmrc configuration, workspace declaration, and lockfile discipline for pnpm projects.

section.title: Installation
section.p: Enable via corepack (Node.js 16.9+): corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate

rule.statement: Pin manager version in root package.json: "packageManager": "pnpm@9.x.y". This ensures corepack picks the same version everywhere — CI, dev, Docker.
rule.rationale: Lockfile format changes between pnpm major versions. Without pinning, different developers and CI use different versions, producing lockfile churn that obscures real dependency changes.

section.title: .npmrc Configuration
section.reference: Strict .npmrc config (engine-strict, frozen lockfile, no shameful hoist)

rule.statement: Keep shamefully-hoist=false (the default). Reject any PR setting shamefully-hoist=true without a 2-line justification. It defeats pnpm's phantom-dependency protection.
rule.rationale: shamefully-hoist=true is the universal agent wrong-fix when a native add-on or bundler can't find a package. The real fix is adding the package as an explicit dependency. Hoisting masks the dependency gap and spreads phantom imports.

section.p: Key .npmrc settings: strict-peer-dependencies=true, auto-install-peers=true, lockfile=true, prefer-frozen-lockfile=true, engine-strict=true.

rule.statement: Always use --frozen-lockfile in CI. Never --no-frozen-lockfile "to make it work". Lockfile drift must be fixed locally, not suppressed in CI.
rule.rationale: --no-frozen-lockfile in CI allows silent dependency drift that breaks reproducible builds. Any drift should be diagnosed and committed locally first.

rule.statement: Commit pnpm-lock.yaml separately from application code changes. This keeps the lockfile diff reviewable and enables targeted revert.
rule.rationale: Mixing lockfile and app changes in one commit makes it impossible to identify which dependency change caused a regression.

section.title: Block Accidental npm/yarn Use
section.p: Add to root package.json scripts: "preinstall": "npx -y only-allow pnpm"
This fails early when npm install or yarn is run accidentally, preventing mixed lockfile corruption.
